title: Grok/xAI provider wiring
description: Add Grok/xAI as the EXISTING openai-compat provider pointed at https://api.x.ai/v1, mirroring the DeepSeek wiring at its three resolution sites, plus a single shared isXaiEndpoint() predicate. NO new ProviderName value, NO new adapter class. Touch-points: (1) src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts SHORTHAND_MAP — add grok shorthands {provider:'openai-compat', modelId} and make the openai-compat endpoint-attach branch select https://api.x.ai/v1 for grok* (vs https://api.deepseek.com for deepseek*); (2) src/providers/factory.ts validateApiKey openai-compat case — add an isXaiEndpoint(endpoint) arm requiring apiKey ?? process.env.XAI_API_KEY and throwing a clear 'configured to use Grok/xAI but XAI_API_KEY is not set' error; (3) src/providers/factory.ts createClient openai-compat case key-injection — add a parallel isXaiEndpoint arm injecting XAI_API_KEY (the OpenAICompatAdapter constructor call is UNCHANGED). Add isXaiEndpoint(endpoint?:string):boolean as the SOLE place the 'api.x.ai' host string is matched, reused by both factory sites (mirror however DeepSeek's host is matched today). src/fleet/index.ts validateManifestCredentials already calls validateApiKey(provider, role, apiKey, endpoint) per child section, so adding the xAI arm makes the fleet pre-spawn credential check recognize Grok with ZERO edits to index.ts. This sprint changes NO fleet behavior and NO tier logic (that is Sprint 2).

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build) and zero typecheck errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).

The full test suite passes with zero new failures (npm run test); the 6 pre-existing tests/e2e/cockpit-integration.test.ts MCP 'Connection closed' failures are the only allowed failures and are confirmed unrelated.

resolveProviderModel('grok') and the other added grok shorthands return { provider:'openai-compat', modelId:<the grok model id>, endpoint:'https://api.x.ai/v1' }, exactly mirroring the deepseek shorthand shape; a collocated test asserts the provider, modelId, and endpoint.

validateApiKey('openai-compat', role, undefined, 'https://api.x.ai/v1') THROWS a message naming Grok/xAI and XAI_API_KEY when neither providerConfig.apiKey nor process.env.XAI_API_KEY is set, and does NOT throw when XAI_API_KEY is present; the existing api.deepseek.com and no-key (Ollama) behaviors are unchanged.

createClient for an openai-compat provider at an api.x.ai endpoint injects XAI_API_KEY into the OpenAICompatAdapter (parallel to the DEEPSEEK_API_KEY arm), verified by a test that asserts the adapter is constructed with the xAI key (and that Ollama/other openai-compat endpoints keep the no-key behavior).

isXaiEndpoint(endpoint) is the ONLY place 'api.x.ai' is matched; a grep/test confirms 'api.x.ai' appears solely inside isXaiEndpoint, and both validateApiKey and createClient call it. ProviderName is unchanged (still anthropic|openai|google|openai-compat|claude-code).
lint passes (npm run lint, zero errors); no new disallowed imports; the change is confined to model-resolver.ts + factory.ts (+ their tests).

nonGoals: Do NOT add a new ProviderName value or a GrokAdapter class — Grok is openai-compat.
Do NOT add tier logic, FleetChild.tier, or any buildChildConfig change (Sprint 2).
Do NOT edit validateManifestCredentials' loop body — the xAI arm in validateApiKey must make it work for free.
Do NOT change the DeepSeek or Ollama openai-compat behavior.
Do NOT hardcode an XAI_API_KEY or commit any secret.
stopConditions: grok* resolves to openai-compat @ api.x.ai/v1, XAI_API_KEY is validated+injected only via the single isXaiEndpoint predicate, ProviderName unchanged, and build/typecheck/test/lint green with no new failures
definitionOfDone: src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts SHORTHAND_MAP has grok shorthands resolving to openai-compat with endpoint https://api.x.ai/v1. src/providers/factory.ts exports/uses isXaiEndpoint(endpoint) and has parallel xAI arms in validateApiKey (require XAI_API_KEY) and createClient (inject XAI_API_KEY), leaving OpenAICompatAdapter and ProviderName unchanged. validateManifestCredentials recognizes xAI with no edit. Collocated tests cover shorthand resolution, key-required/throw, key-injection, the single-predicate invariant, and DeepSeek/Ollama non-regression. Build/typecheck/test/lint green.
assumptions: xAI exposes an OpenAI-wire-compatible API at https://api.x.ai/v1, so OpenAICompatAdapter handles it unchanged (research:50).
The DeepSeek wiring (SHORTHAND_MAP deepseek entry + endpoint attach at model-resolver.ts; validateApiKey + createClient api.deepseek.com arms in factory.ts:251-255) is the exact template to mirror.

The grok model id default(s) can be a placeholder shorthand (e.g. 'grok-4' / 'grok-4-fast'); the actual id is overridable via child.config.model and is not asserted to be a live model — tests assert routing/endpoint/key, not a live API call.
validateManifestCredentials (src/fleet/index.ts) already passes section.endpoint into validateApiKey, so the xAI arm is sufficient for the fleet pre-check.
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST: src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts (SHORTHAND_MAP ~:11-22 and the resolveProviderModel openai-compat endpoint-attach branch ~:79-85 where deepseek gets endpoint 'https://api.deepseek.com' — you add a selector so grok* gets 'https://api.x.ai/v1'); src/providers/factory.ts (validateApiKey openai-compat case ~:128 with the `endpoint?.includes('api.deepseek.com')` check ~:130, and createClient openai-compat case ~:244-261 with the DEEPSEEK_API_KEY injection ~:251-255). Mirror BOTH deepseek arms for xAI.

Add `export function isXaiEndpoint(endpoint?: string): boolean { return !!endpoint && endpoint.includes('api.x.ai'); }` in factory.ts (or a small shared spot factory imports) and use it in BOTH the validateApiKey arm and the createClient arm — do not inline the substring twice. Optionally refactor the existing deepseek match into an isDeepseekEndpoint() for symmetry, but that is not required.

SHORTHAND_MAP: add `grok: { provider: 'openai-compat', modelId: 'grok-4' }` and a fast variant (e.g. `'grok-4-fast'`); ensure the openai-compat endpoint-attach branch returns endpoint 'https://api.x.ai/v1' when the modelId is a grok id, else keeps 'https://api.deepseek.com'. Keep it a small branch keyed on the modelId/shorthand.

validateApiKey: in the openai-compat case, BEFORE/alongside the deepseek check add: `if (isXaiEndpoint(endpoint)) { const key = apiKey ?? process.env['XAI_API_KEY']; if (!key) throw new Error(`${roleLabel} is configured to use Grok/xAI but XAI_API_KEY is not set. Set the XAI_API_KEY environment variable and try again.`); }`. createClient: in the openai-compat case, extend the key resolution to `apiKey ?? (isXaiEndpoint(resolvedEndpoint) ? process.env['XAI_API_KEY'] : resolvedEndpoint.includes('api.deepseek.com') ? process.env['DEEPSEEK_API_KEY'] : undefined)`; pass to the UNCHANGED OpenAICompatAdapter ctor.

Tests (collocated *.test.ts next to each file): model-resolver grok resolution; factory validateApiKey xAI throw/no-throw (set/unset XAI_API_KEY via vi.stubEnv or by passing apiKey) + deepseek+ollama unchanged; createClient xAI key injection (you may need to assert via a spy on the adapter constructor or via the resolved key — follow how the existing deepseek createClient test asserts, if any; otherwise assert validateApiKey behavior + that createClient does not throw with XAI_API_KEY set). isXaiEndpoint single-source: a test importing isXaiEndpoint + a grep-style assertion. Follow ESM .js imports, import type, unicode section headers.
evaluatorNotes: sc-1-3: call resolveProviderModel('grok') and any other added ids; assert {provider:'openai-compat', endpoint:'https://api.x.ai/v1', modelId set}. sc-1-4: call validateApiKey('openai-compat','generator',undefined,'https://api.x.ai/v1') with XAI_API_KEY unset (vi.stubEnv) → expect throw naming XAI_API_KEY; with it set → no throw; confirm api.deepseek.com still requires DEEPSEEK_API_KEY and a bare Ollama endpoint still needs no key. sc-1-5: assert createClient injects the xAI key (spy/adapter-arg or no-throw-with-key) and Ollama keeps no-key. sc-1-6: grep the repo for 'api.x.ai' and confirm it appears ONLY in isXaiEndpoint; confirm ProviderName union literal is unchanged. sc-1-2: run the FULL suite; only the 6 known cockpit-integration MCP failures may fail; assert no model-resolver/factory/fleet test regressed. Build+typecheck+lint clean.
